regulatoryDomain: Supply Chain Resilience / Quality Risk Management
lawRegulationFrameworkStandardName: FD&C Act § 506C(j) (amended by CARES Act)
level4DetailedRequirement: Requires manufacturers of life-supporting or life-sustaining drugs to develop, maintain, and implement redundancy Risk Management Plans (RMPs) to evaluate risks to the drug supply [1, 2].
level5GranularObligation: Develop, approve, maintain, review, and implement a product-specific RMP with funded mitigations.
exactRequirementOrSourceLanguage: Requires manufacturers of life-supporting or life-sustaining drugs to develop, maintain, and implement redundancy Risk Management Plans (RMPs) to evaluate risks to the drug supply [1, 2].
plainEnglishInterpretation: The company must proactively map supply risks, define mitigations, and keep evidence that the plan is current and implemented.
whoMustComply: Drug manufacturers, product owners, quality unit, CMOs, and associated contract facilities
exampleBusinessOwner: Regulatory Affairs Lead
operationalProcessImpacted: Supply risk management, S&OP, inventory planning, continuity planning
requiredAction: Develop, approve, maintain, review, and implement a product-specific RMP with funded mitigations.
requiredControl: Approved RMP SOP, risk scoring method, mitigation owner tracking, annual/event review
requiredEvidenceDocumentation: Approved RMP, risk register, vulnerability assessment, mitigation plan, management review record
reportingRequirement: Regulatory Affairs notification to FDA within 5 business days if applicable shortage/discontinuance threshold is met.
monitoringRequirement: Review risk indicators, supplier performance, inventory, capacity, quality events, and shortage triggers
applicabilityConditions: Applicable to life-supporting/life-sustaining drugs and drugs for debilitating diseases, as described in source.
dependencies: Supplier data; inventory policy; quality history; capacity data; finance funding
potentialConflictsOrAmbiguities: No conflict identified in source; validate with legal/compliance before implementation.
riskOfNonCompliance: Drug shortage, inability to supply patients, FDA inspection observation, management review/CAPA exposure.
implementationNotes: Embed in management review; connect risk scoring to funded mitigations and change control triggers.
practicalExampleForBusinessLeader: A sole-source API is identified as high risk; the business either qualifies a second source or funds safety stock and tracks the mitigation.
